headline: 467 airframes grounded across 25 states creating $50M–$2B national exposure as propeller supply chain shows 96% China dependency with no domestic alternatives in tracked inventory.

lead_story.title: NASAO Grounding Event Reaches 467 Airframes Across 25 States
lead_story.body: Oregon/NASAO Revision 2 white paper survey confirms 467 drone airframes are grounded or operationally restricted across 23 states, with 25 state DOTs reporting exposure. National replacement exposure ranges from $50M to $2B depending on replacement pathway and compliance interpretation. This represents the largest documented Blue UAS compliance-driven grounding event to date. The grounding is creating immediate operational gaps in state infrastructure inspection, emergency response, and survey missions.
lead_story.action: Conduct airframe inventory audit by May 9 to identify NASAO-affected platforms and quantify replacement budget exposure; prioritize mission-critical units for Q3 procurement action before FY2026 budget closeout.

supply_chain.signal: 463 of 484 tracked propeller models (96%) are Chinese-manufactured, with dominant brands HQProp, Gemfan, and DAL all based in Shenzhen/Guangdong. Zero domestic alternatives identified in current parts database.
supply_chain.window: Immediate exposure — propellers are consumables with 30-90 day replacement cycles under operational use.
supply_chain.action: Issue RFI by May 15 for domestic propeller manufacturing capacity; establish 6-month strategic reserve for critical missions; identify NATO-sourced alternatives (Czech, Polish manufacturers) as interim hedge.

supply_chain.signal: 212 of 303 tracked motors (70%) are Chinese-manufactured. T-Motor/Tiger Motors dominates with 62 models from Nan'an City, Fujian. iFlight and BrotherHobby constitute secondary exposure.
supply_chain.window: 6-12 months — motors are durable components but China dependency creates strategic vulnerability for new builds and major overhauls.
supply_chain.action: Qualify at least two non-PRC motor suppliers by Q3 2026 for new procurement specifications; map existing inventory to identify platforms with highest PRC motor concentration.

supply_chain.signal: 187 of 193 tracked FPV cameras (97%) are Chinese-manufactured. FPV cameras are NDAA critical components due to imaging, data storage, and transmission capabilities — not exempt as passive components.
supply_chain.window: 90-180 days — FPV systems are being integrated into tactical and reconnaissance platforms under accelerated timelines.
supply_chain.action: Halt new FPV camera procurements from PRC sources immediately; issue urgent RFP for NDAA-compliant imaging solutions with June 15 response deadline; validate existing inventory against Section 848/889 compliance.

supply_chain.signal: GUR and Ukrainian military intelligence confirm Raspberry Pi 4B as core processor in Geran-2/3 'Borscht Tracker V3' navigation system, with 40,000+ units estimated diverted to Russian Shahed-136/Geran-2 production.
supply_chain.window: Ongoing — diversion has already occurred at scale; secondary procurement controls needed to prevent future hemorrhaging.
supply_chain.action: Implement end-use certification requirements for all single-board computer purchases over 10 units; coordinate with export control compliance to flag bulk RPi orders for secondary review before fulfillment.

gray_zone.status: Texas AG filed 7-count DTPA lawsuit February 18, 2026 in Collin County; no response filed by Anzu as of April 4 — case frozen 45+ days.
gray_zone.development: Teardown analysis confirms PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise, with matching board part numbers. Controller RRC01 is relabeled DJI hardware. Firmware is signed and encrypted with DJI cryptographic keys, with DJI retaining root key control and SDK foundation. Product discontinuation confirmed due to DJI P1 chipset embargo enforcement creating adversary component shortage in Q2 2026.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Federal, state, and local agencies that procured Anzu Raptor/Apex platforms under Blue UAS equivalency assumptions face compliance risk. Texas is seeking injunctive relief and civil penalties up to $10K per violation.
gray_zone.action: Agencies with Anzu platforms should conduct immediate legal and technical review by May 15; prepare budget justification for replacement procurement; do not execute new Anzu purchase orders pending lawsuit resolution.

gray_zone.status: FCC Covered List designation active; existing S1 ($359) and X1 ($599) inventory still available at retail despite firmware prohibition approaching January 1, 2027.
gray_zone.development: Technical analysis shows SkyRover app connects directly to DJI cloud infrastructure with identical encryption keys. OTA update pipeline touches adversary infrastructure. Shell company structure identified: SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co Ltd. Gray zone risk score 0.850 (CRITICAL) — highest among non-DJI entities.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: State and local buyers who purchased SkyRover for budget-constrained missions face operational cliff on January 1, 2027 when firmware updates become prohibited under FCC Covered List enforcement.
gray_zone.action: Immediately cease SkyRover procurement; existing operators should plan 6-month transition timeline to compliant platforms before firmware prohibition takes effect; do not rely on existing inventory beyond Q4 2026.

watch_list.why: Creates operational cliff for all gray zone platforms still in service — no firmware updates, security patches, or feature enhancements permitted after deadline.
watch_list.trigger: FCC denial of DJI/Autel reconsideration petitions (expected Q3 2026) would eliminate last path to regulatory relief and force immediate transition planning.

watch_list.why: FCC retroactively revoked certain pre-ban Autel authorizations in January 2026; Autel reconsideration petition pending with no timeline for decision.
watch_list.trigger: FCC rejection of Autel reconsideration would trigger immediate compliance review for federal buyers using EVO series platforms under NDAA carve-outs.

watch_list.why: Ukrainian drone units conducting concentrated strikes against Russian S-400 and Pantsir-S systems — operational lessons on counter-UAS vulnerability and saturation tactics directly applicable to Blue force planning.
watch_list.trigger: Documented destruction of 5+ strategic air defense systems in single operational phase would validate swarm doctrine and accelerate DoD small-UAS procurement timelines.

predictions.event: Anzu Robotics product discontinuation due to DJI P1 OCU chipset embargo enforcement
predictions.hedge: Establish pre-approved vendor list of 3+ non-gray-zone alternatives by May 31; pre-position Q3/Q4 procurement vehicles to enable rapid transition when Anzu inventory exhausts.

predictions.event: FCC Covered List firmware prohibition creates operational cliff for gray zone platforms still in service
predictions.hedge: Complete platform migration planning by June 30; allocate FY2027 budget for full gray zone fleet replacement; do not plan missions dependent on gray zone platforms beyond December 2026.

predictions.event: Gray zone entity risk scoring stability masks accelerating regulatory enforcement — FCC equipment authorization freeze understates true procurement risk
predictions.hedge: Do not interpret stable gray zone scores as risk plateau; treat all entities with score >0.50 as non-procurable regardless of score delta; require legal sign-off on any gray zone purchase over $10K.

signal_summary.top_concern: 96% China propeller dependency with zero domestic alternatives
